scenarios.title: Sandwich shop owner
scenarios.scenario: You are working at a sandwich shop. The owner has asked you to update the sales spreadsheet and the customer flyer.
scenarios.briefHtml:
From: sam@pearson.com
Subject: Sandwich shop updates

Please update SANDWICH SALES to show totals, income and a chart. Also update the SANDWICH FLYER to a landscape layout with a table of offers and an image.

tasks.label: A1
tasks.brief: Search online for the postal address of the Food Standards Agency in London.
tasks.prompt: Use search terms and record the address you found.

tasks.label: A2
tasks.brief: Set up an automated signature in your email composer that includes 'Kind regards' and your name.

tasks.label: A3
tasks.brief: Send an email to sam@pearson.com with the postal address of the Food Standards Agency. Include a subject line, greeting and close, and the address in the message.

tasks.label: B1
tasks.brief: Open the flyer. Set it to landscape, add the list of special offers next to or below 'Special offers this month', and format it as a table.
tasks.initialHtml:
Sam's Sandwich Shop

Special offers this month:

(insert list of special offers as a table below this line)

Visit us at 12 High Street.

tasks.label: B2
tasks.brief: Format the heading text (change the colour), add a page border, and make sure the flyer fits the page without images overlapping text.

tasks.label: C1
tasks.brief: Open SANDWICH SALES. Merge cells A1:H1 with the title 'Sandwich Sales' centred, font size 24, and shade with a light green colour. Add formulae for Total sold and Income in columns G and H, including an Average row.

tasks.label: C2
tasks.brief: Calculate the Average sold (row 12) for each week. Format the currency columns as £ with 2 decimal places. Sort A3:F11 in ascending order of Price.

tasks.label: C3
tasks.brief: Insert a chart (bar, column or pie — NOT a line graph). Labels: column A. Values: column G. Add a chart title and axis labels. Place the chart on a separate worksheet.

scenarios.title: Utility meter reading
scenarios.scenario: You need to submit an electricity meter reading using an online portal.
scenarios.briefHtml:
From: billing@samsdfs.biz

Please use the online form to submit your meter reading. Account: R753197 · Reading: 032075 · Postcode: NT25 3BG · Phone: 01632 960455 · Email: sam@pearson.com. Choose Email and Text as marketing preferences.

tasks.label: D1
tasks.brief: Complete the meter reading form with the details from the brief, select Electricity, enter the reading, upload an image as evidence and tick Email + Text marketing preferences.

scenarios.title: Wireless printer problem
scenarios.scenario: You can't get your wireless printer to connect. Use online resources to find a solution and file the information.
scenarios.briefHtml: Create a new folder called Devices. Inside it, create a subfolder called Printer. Save your research in a suitably named file.

tasks.label: E1
tasks.brief: Create the Devices folder and the Printer subfolder.

tasks.label: E2
tasks.brief: Search for a solution to a wireless printer connection problem. Record the search you used and the information you found. Note whether the source looks reliable and up to date.
tasks.prompt: Enter search terms and record the finding.

scenarios.title: Manage your online account
scenarios.scenario: You want to update your details and preferences for an online shop.
scenarios.briefHtml: Go to your account settings. Update your phone number. Set your marketing preferences to Email only. Change your password to a strong one.

tasks.label: F1
tasks.brief: Complete the account settings form below.

fields.name: password
fields.label: New password
fields.help: Must include letters, numbers and a symbol.

tasks.label: F2
tasks.brief: Take a screenshot of the completed form showing your password is hidden with dots.
tasks.prompt: Upload a screenshot of the completed form.

scenarios.title: Community group newsletter
scenarios.scenario: You volunteer for a community group. Edit their newsletter and email it to members.
scenarios.briefHtml: Open the newsletter. Add a heading in a clear colour. Insert a small image. Make the layout landscape. Add a page border. Then email it to members with a friendly subject and message.

tasks.label: B1
tasks.brief: Edit the newsletter as described in the brief.
tasks.initialHtml:
Community News

Welcome to this month's newsletter.

Insert image and special content here.

images.src: data:image/svg+xml;utf8,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='160' height='100'%3E%3Crect width='100%25' height='100%25' fill='%23f0801f'/%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' text-anchor='middle' fill='white' font-size='18'%3ECommunity%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E

tasks.label: B2
tasks.brief: Email the finished newsletter to members@samsdfs.biz with a friendly subject and greeting, and attach the newsletter (use the Attach file button after editing the document).

scenarios.title: Fitness tracker comparison
scenarios.scenario: You want to buy a fitness tracker and need to choose between two brands.
scenarios.briefHtml: Research two fitness trackers, compare them in a spreadsheet (price, battery life, features), then send your recommendation by email.

tasks.label: C1
tasks.brief: Complete the comparison spreadsheet. Use a formula to calculate the difference in price.

tasks.label: C2
tasks.brief: Email your recommendation to friend@samsdfs.biz with a clear subject and a greeting. Include which tracker you would buy and why.

scenarios.title: Charity event report
scenarios.scenario: You volunteered at a charity bake sale and need to write a one-page report and circulate it to the committee.
scenarios.briefHtml: Create a one-page report including a heading in a clear colour, a table of items and totals, a small image, and a page border. Email the finished report to committee@samsdfs.biz with a clear subject.

tasks.label: C1
tasks.brief: Open the sales spreadsheet. Calculate the total income for each item (price × sold), then a grand total in cell D9. Format currency to 2 decimal places.

tasks.label: B1
tasks.brief: Edit the one-page report. Add a coloured heading, paste the totals into the document, add a page border and a small image.
tasks.initialHtml:
Bake Sale Report

Insert overview here. State the grand total raised and any highlights.

images.src: data:image/svg+xml;utf8,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='180' height='100'%3E%3Crect width='100%25' height='100%25' fill='%23f9c2d4'/%3E%3Ctext x='50%25' y='55%25' text-anchor='middle' fill='%23a23a6c' font-size='22'%3EBake Sale%3C/text%3E%3C/svg%3E

tasks.label: B2
tasks.brief: Email the report to committee@samsdfs.biz. Attach the document, write a clear subject and a polite message.

scenarios.title: Café staff rota
scenarios.scenario: You're a supervisor at a café. Build the staff rota for the week and email it to the manager.
scenarios.briefHtml: Open the rota. Add hours for each member of staff, calculate a Total per person and a Total per day. Format the title and sort by total hours descending. Email the rota to manager@samsdfs.biz with the file attached.

tasks.label: C1
tasks.brief: Complete the rota. Add total hours for each person (column H, =SUM of Mon..Sat) and column totals (row 11).

tasks.label: C2
tasks.brief: Email manager@samsdfs.biz with the rota attached. Use a sensible subject. Mention the highest-hours staff member in the body.

scenarios.title: Cloud storage for the team
scenarios.scenario: Your team needs to share project files in the cloud. Plan the folder structure, write a short procedure for new starters, and email it round.
scenarios.briefHtml: Create a sensible cloud folder structure for the team project (e.g. Project/Year/Topic). Write a short Word-style guide explaining how new starters should organise their files. Email the guide to team@samsdfs.biz.

tasks.label: E1
tasks.brief: Create the folder structure in the file management area: a top-level Project folder, with two subfolders (e.g. 'Reports' and 'Photos').

tasks.label: B1
tasks.brief: Write a short procedure (at least 4 sentences) explaining how new team members should name their files and where to put them. Use a heading, a bullet list and bold for key terms.

tasks.label: B2
tasks.brief: Email the procedure to team@samsdfs.biz with a friendly subject and a polite close.

scenarios.title: Suspicious email investigation
scenarios.scenario: Your manager forwards you a suspicious-looking email and asks you to advise whether it is genuine.
scenarios.briefHtml:
Forwarded message: 'Dear Customer, your account has been suspended. Click here to verify your details: http://yourbank-secure-verify.xyz/login'

Research the signs of phishing, write a short note explaining what is suspicious, and email your manager (manager@samsdfs.biz) with your advice.

tasks.label: A1
tasks.brief: Search online for guidance on spotting phishing emails. Record at least two warning signs.
tasks.prompt: Enter search terms and record what you found.

tasks.label: B1
tasks.brief: Write a short note (at least 5 sentences) listing the warning signs in the email and what your manager should do about it. Use a bullet list.

tasks.label: A2
tasks.brief: Email manager@samsdfs.biz with your advice and attach the note. Sensible subject, greeting and close.
